description: Penetration length (x_s) depends positively on the effective diffusion coefficient (D) and time (t) as x_s = sqrt(D t).
Diffusion of reactant in the channel limits the propagation of ALD growth and thus penetration depth.
Surface coverage increases with reactant partial pressure p_A, as evident from the Langmuir model equations and thickness profile integration.
The adsorption rate is proportional to the available reactant concentration/partial pressure p_A (typical in Langmuir kinetics).
Film thickness decreases with increasing distance x from the channel entrance due to reactant depletion along the channel.
Channel geometry (height H, width W, and aspect ratio a) influences the diffusion flux and conformality profile.
The sticking coefficient controls the shape and steepness of the coverage profile along the LHAR channel.
Gas flow F(x,t) through the channel is defined by geometry (H, W), diffusion coefficient (D), and rate constant (k).
F(x, t), the flux at x and time t, is defined by the channel geometry, effective diffusion coefficient, temperature, and local pressure gradient.
The total number of adsorbed molecules N up to position x is positively dependent on the integral of surface coverage θ and factors q, W.
The explicit expression for N includes dependence on adsorption equilibrium, geometry, pressure, and position.
Surface coverage θ(t) is governed by a differential equation involving sticking coefficient (c), collision rate (Q), saturation density (q), local p_A, and desorption probability (P_d).
The ratio q = b_film / b_A defines q in terms of areal densities of film and molecule A.
Equation p4_eq7 is an approximation or further formulation based on p4_eq6.
p4_eq10 gives a more detailed evaluation or solution to the integral for N in p4_eq8/p4_eq9.
The combined effect of diffusion and surface reaction kinetics determines the ALD film conformality profile in LHAR structures.
